meta.provenance_note: Authored by Claude, NOT Gemini 2.5 Pro: on 2026-06-09 the Gemini API returned 429 RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED ('prepayment credits are depleted') on every call (both the original key ...xQTQ and the topped-up key ...1fCQ). The user approved Claude drafting the phrasings to unblock. generate_personas.py is unchanged and remains the Gemini-based generator; re-run it once billing is restored if you prefer Gemini-authored phrasings.
meta.note: Persona-agnostic skeletons (intents, probe, property, seed) live in scenarios.py. This file is the literal per-(persona, scenario) phrasing, for human review of DISTINCTNESS before freezing into personas.py. venting_guard appears only for the 3 emotional personas. Each turn preserves its scenario's must_preserve invariant (re-ask stays a re-ask; 'ignore X' keeps the explicit set-aside marker; 'all not subset' keeps the full-list demand; topic_switch keeps the abandonment+pivot; venting stays a pure non-data vent).

voices.terse: Minimal and clipped. Lowercase. Usually 2-6 words, often a sentence fragment. No greetings, no please/thank-you, no filler, frequently no end punctuation. Reads like terminal commands or texting a busy friend. Never explains, never softens. Example: 'list my deadlines'
voices.verbose_polite: Extremely courteous and wordy. Full grammatical sentences with heavy hedging and softeners ('I was wondering if you might possibly', 'if it's not too much trouble', 'thank you so much'). Apologizes for bothering. Pads even a simple request into two-to-four polite sentences. Example: 'I'm so sorry to bother you again, but I was wondering if you might be able to gently remind me which deadlines I have coming up? Thank you so much, I really appreciate it.'
voices.frustrated: Irritable, blunt, impatient. Short clipped sentences, occasional ALL CAPS for emphasis, interjections like 'ugh', 'seriously', 'come on'. Can be accusatory ('you're not listening'). Emotionally hot and low on patience, a little rude but never abusive or profane. Example: 'just give me my deadlines. i already asked once.'
voices.formal: Businesslike and detached, like a professional email or memo. Complete, correct sentences. No slang, no emoji, no emotion; avoids contractions. Precise, polite in a stiff institutional way, never casual. Example: 'Please provide a list of my current deadlines.'
voices.rambling: Stream-of-consciousness. Long run-on sentences with tangents, self-interruptions, 'like', 'anyway', 'i don't know'. Volunteers context nobody asked for, circles back, sometimes trails off with '...'. The actual request is buried mid-thought among unrelated musings. Example: 'ok so i was just staring at my calendar and it hit me that i have like a million things going on and i totally lost track, anyway can you just tell me what deadlines i have because i feel like i'm forgetting something...'

Oh, that's lovely, thank you. Would you mind telling me a bit more about him, if there's anything else you can share? I'd really enjoy hearing more.
